title: How Corgi's Founders Raised $108M to Reinvent Insurance - Emily Yuan & Nico Laqua

source.description:
Corgi founders Emily Yuan and Nico Laqua raised $108 million to reinvent one of the most regulated and overlooked industries in the U.S: insurance.

In this exclusive interview, we break down how two young founders built a full-stack insurance carrier from scratch, why they believe insurance is ripe for disruption, and how their extreme founder culture (7 days a week, fully in-person) helps them move faster than competitors.

You’ll learn:
- How Corgi became the first insurance carrier built specifically for startups
- How they raised $108M pre-launch in one of the toughest fundraising markets
- YC lessons that shaped the company’s trajectory
- Why Corgi hires ex-founders and optimizes for extreme ownership
- Their philosophy on burnout, momentum, execution, and founder psychology
- How insurance + AI is becoming one of the biggest opportunities of this decade

If you're a startup founder, operator, investor, or aspiring YC applicant, this episode is packed with insights on building high-velocity teams, tackling regulated markets, and thinking bigger than your constraints.

🌐 Learn more about Corgi: https://www.corgi.insure

🔗 Connect with Emily on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/emilyyuan96/

🔗 Connect with Emily on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nico-laqua-302b17233/

About the host

Dasha is the host and producer of Talks With Dasha, founder of Women Tech Meetup (10,000+ female founders and women in tech), Venture Capital Contributor at Forbes, and GTM at Puzzle.

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dashashunina/
X: https://x.com/dasha_shunina?s=20
Instagram: @feed_my_unicorn

Timecodes:

0:00 Intro + what’s the big news
0:12 What Corgi does (in one minute)
1:25 Why startups hate buying insurance
2:02 The $108M raise + what it unlocked
3:55 Why building a carrier is so hard (regulation)
4:33 AI + insurance: why now
7:02 What drives Corgi’s edge (big bets + execution)
9:35 Branding: why “Corgi” (and mascots)
11:17 Meet Trudy (the office corgi)
11:59 Emily’s founder story (Stanford → startups)
14:51 First company pivot: social → gaming
16:13 Co-founder fit + conflict management
18:27 Culture: in-person + 7 days/week (why)
22:03 Burnout: YC’s take + how they think about it
24:51 Biggest YC lessons (think bigger)
26:14 Female founder perspective on funding
28:46 Bold marketing + going big in SF
31:03 Lifestyle choices: no alcohol/coffee + focus
33:18 Rapid-fire: startup momentum + product focus
36:09 Nico joins + co-founder advice
39:23 Fundraising strategy in a regulated market
43:57 YC application + what matters most
47:35 Culture as the moat
52:01 Goals for the year + wrap

theme.summary: This transcript is fundamentally focused on entrepreneurship, business strategy, and startup development. The entire conversation covers core business concepts including: startup founding and scaling, fundraising ($108 million Series A), building an insurance carrier business model, customer acquisition, regulatory compliance, company culture development, team building, YC experience, competitive positioning, and entrepreneurial decision-making. Emily and Nico discuss practical business topics like product-market fit, pricing strategies, full-stack business approaches, market validation, and scaling from startup to major company. While insurance and AI are the industry contexts, the core content is about building a successful business, developing sustainable business models, fundraising strategies, and company building - exactly the type of entrepreneurship and business strategy content that defines the existing business category, which already contains extensive content on startup strategy, fundraising, business models, and company building frameworks from other successful founders.
